title: Ayer Electric<>Beam AI - Next Steps - Apr 08, 2026
transcript:
Anthony Anilesh Antic: Being on this call, we've processed the output. The output is ready and on call here. I have Dhamni, who you've already met, and we also have Rachana here. So like I mentioned, our process has verification in terms of a human layer as well. Right. To be able to ensure if the instructions given by the lead, the customer, in this case, match the output that the AI has to measure. And this we do because output is very much driven by input. So in case there are certain sort of discrepancies and in this case there have been certain amount of predictions and assumptions that have been made by our AI. So firstly, what I'd like to understand is for this particular takeoff, if there are. If there's any sort of thoughts from your end saying that, hey, I thought that maybe AI would find it difficult to measure, I don't know, abc, xyz, whatever that might be. And right now as well, in terms of your process and how you do takeoffs, if you. If you feel that these are certain areas of difficulty for me, please let us know so that Dhamni will run you through those exact bits on the takeoff. Hello?

Caitlin Ayer: I think Max might have froze. He's our estimating expert on the team. Maybe let's just give him a second to sort out his connection.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: So does Max have a few estimators under him or is he literally the one man army here?

Caitlin Ayer: We have a, like a full estimating team.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Got it.

Caitlin Ayer: Or right now, but.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Okay, so among the four estimators, what is the total number of takeoffs that you guys are bidding on a monthly basis from a takeoff perspective?

Caitlin Ayer: I don't know, maybe five. We do like a lot of different type of estimating. So.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Wait, so hold on. So among the four estimators, each one of them are working on one takeoff the entire month.

Caitlin Ayer: No, we don't have. Not all of our projects require takeoffs. It's more of like the complex commercial side of things. So now that's not like all inclusive of everything that we're bidding. It's just not. Every type of project that we do requires like an intense takeoff. So this is a tool we're looking to use just for those specific types of projects that we're bidding. Hey, Max. Looks like you're back. I'm back.

Max Carpenter: I apologize for that. My Internet just crashed and everything stopped. So.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Can you hear me? I mean, would you get. So basically what I was trying to say is if there's any area that you feel would have been difficult for AI to measure, or any area of difficulty for you to do takeoffs as well, that you can let us know so that DHAMNI will run you through those specific bits on the takeoff.

Max Carpenter: I wouldn't. I don't know if there's necessary necessarily areas that I would say would be specifically difficult for us to measure. I think a lot of this that we were hoping would be, you know, some of that kind of tedious tasks and for AI to be using as a time saver. So I don't know if there's one specific area. I think it's more of just looking at what that overall accuracy level is and seeing if that time saver would be worth it.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Got it done. Yeah, I think we've got what we wanted. So, Damani. Yes. Over to you.

Damini Gorania: Thanks, animesh. There we go. So, Katelyn and Max, as you already know, this is what our software looks like. I hope you got the chance to go through it. So I'll just quickly show you how you can navigate through the sheets and all the details which has been captured by AI. So I'll just go through the sheet. As you can see, all the line items you'll be able to see on the left hand tab, the quantities, the description, everything related to that will be available on this tab. You'll be able to see the sheets in the center and whichever sheet you would like to see, you can select it from here. There will be. There might be multiple traits, but in this case we just have electrical. So there we go. All the sheets are for electrical. I have selected this sheet. We can. Another feature we have is this one is paid specific and another one is called takeoff aggregate. So if you are staying on page specific, all the items description, all the quantities you are seeing over here on the measurement tab will be for this particular sheet only, which is selected on the plan step. But if you are going on takeoff aggregate, you'll be able to see a summarized version for entire project. All the items which has been captured for this project with the quantity and description, you'll be able to see it over here. Additional feature we have under Takeoff Aggregators as once we enlarge any particular line item, as you can see, I have enlarged a telephone outlet. You'll be able to see a feature which says present on. So this will show you on which many or how many particular sheets that line item is present on. So as you can see, it's on E11, E100 and E 1.01. So if you click on any of that hyperlink, it will directly take you to that sheet and that line item. So as you can see we are on the sheet which I just clicked, that is E101 and then it's Telephone outlet that has been highlighted. And then once you click on this locate measurement button over here, you'll be able to locate that exactly where that is located on the sheet. As you can see, it's also getting highlighted in red. You can always zoom in zoom out to see the exact location. Export button over here. So once you'll get live, you'll have your designated account from which you'll be able to submit the jobs. And once you have your own account, you'll be also able to export this takeoff into various formats. First one we have is Shareable link. So in Shareable link you'll be able to share whatever you are seeing right now. Only this Edit takeoff feature and this export and resubmit button won't be available. So that will be just a view only link. So with whomever you are sharing that link with, they'll be able to navigate through the software, they'll be able to see where different line items are located, the description and quantities of those as well, but they won't be able to export it by themselves. They can also share that shareable link with any other person. Then next we have is PDF and Excel export. I'll show you how the Excel looks once we are done with the software walkthrough. And you'll be also able to export it into PDF format as well. We have various export layouts. You can choose the size as per your preference. We have different options. You can either include the description, exclude the description as well as the material, and you can always download the report from here, or you can just send it to email. Any questions till now, team?

Max Carpenter: I don't believe so. I think that that all makes sense.

Damini Gorania: Perfect. As you can see over here, if there are any particular notes, assumption or any information which AI want to give you while you are reviewing the project, it will be mentioned like this. It says we have considered Cat 6 cable for all the telecommunication outlets, just in case if there has been any assumptions which might have been made during the takeoff, that will be also mentioned like this, either here or on the Excel sheet. But they all will be highlighted. So whenever you are either reviewing the takeoff or submitting it, you'll be able to see all the notes or the information which you require to see while submitting it. Okay, perfect. Then I'll just go through this. So as you can see, there are multiple items which has been captured on this sheet. If you would like to see the description or detail of any particular item, you can always on this click button over here. That way it will enlarge that particular item. As you can see this one is Cat 6 cable for computer outlet. As I mentioned we have a locate measurement button over here. You can always locate that. That way it will show you the exact location of that line item. It also gives you the length which is 3,955ft. Second we have a Cat 6 cable as well. Again you can locate that. And similarly we have telephone outlet cable which is located over here. Same procedure. You can click on the plus button. That way you'll be able to see the description. There are no description on this one, but yeah, there we go. Good example of description. On this sheet we have different feathers. Sorry feeders. First one is this. You can always click here. That way as you can see here the description is given over here the name of that entire line item. It gives you the detail of that feeder. Again you can always locate it. You can zoom in, zoom out. You can also hover over to that item to see the actual length of that item, the entire name and the description related with that under which group that item is and everything. Are we good? Tim? All any questions on how to navigate through the software to see where any different item is located or anything related to that?

Max Carpenter: I don't think so. It seems pretty straightforward to be able to navigate and do all that.

Damini Gorania: Perfect. Okay, so I'll jump directly to the Excel. So as I mentioned you can export it from here, you can download it or email. I'll just quickly show you. So once you download the Excel report, this is something how will look like you'll always have your item over here we have categorized them under electrical, lighting and different like lightning wiring, power devices etc. You'll be able to see the entire line item name over here. If there are any description related to that line item will be also mentioned over here. Quantity and the unit in which the quantity has been captured and the present on sheet. So you can also see which or how many different sheets that line item is present on. All the quantities and description. So any questions or concern regarding the Excel output team?

Max Carpenter: I don't think so. That that's some good information to have. It looks good.

Damini Gorania: Perfect. So I think Anilish, I'm good with the walkthrough if you would like to continue from here.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: So Caitlin, Max, here's what I'm gonna do. I'll send you an email along with the link to the platform. So basically damni just that export shareable link, just copy paste it in the meeting chat here. So the link that's being pasted in the meeting chat here, that same link I will share with you over email and I will share the Excel sheet, which is the bill of materials as well. So you can have a check and get back to me. So ideally, when do you think it makes sense for us to sort of reconvene?

Caitlin Ayer: I'm not sure.

Max Carpenter: I mean I know obviously as a team we're gonna have to go over this and talk, so it'll. It'll take a little bit. Kaylin, what are your thoughts on that?

Caitlin Ayer: I'd say would need at least a week just to do our own internal review against our own output and then review with our larger team. Maybe we could aim for like, I don't know, really look at the calendar. Maybe like the first week of April. That could give us enough time to internally review and talk about potential next steps.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Okay, how about does Wednesday 8th of April work or 6th? Actually 7th. 7th work slime for me.

Max Carpenter: That works for me.

Caitlin Ayer: I can't do the seventh. The eighth would be best for me. If not, I'm more flexible later that week with the vapor.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Same time, what, 11?

Caitlin Ayer: Yep, that's fine for me.

Max Carpenter: Understood.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: I have moved. Okay, so I moved our meeting from today to the 8th of April. I have just sent you the meeting invite. Can you let me know if you've received it?

Caitlin Ayer: Still thinking on my end.

Max Carpenter: Yeah, I haven't gotten anything yet.

Caitlin Ayer: Just got it.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Great. Sir, in terms of the internal review, so ideally it's going to take a look at accuracy. Is there anything else that you would be reviewing? Something else that we can help you with? The queries that you see popping up that we can address on this call?

Caitlin Ayer: I don't think so if we have questions or things along the way, is it okay if we just reply to our email thread and go from there?

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Works completely fine. Great. All right, Perfect, perfect. So yeah, let me send you that email and yeah, I will. In fact, Kelly, I'll just send up a follow up email sometime next week or the week after that. Just. Yeah, yeah, I think towards the end of next week, by 26th or by 30th, I'll send you a follow up email to check on progress and yeah, we can from there because in case you guys tend to review this earlier then. Yeah, okay. All right. Makes a lot of sense. Anything else that you might need help with? Anything at all that you can think of?

Max Carpenter: I don't think so.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Great.

Caitlin Ayer: All right.

Anthony Anilesh Antic: Perfect. Done. All right, Max, Caitlin, thank you so much for your time. I will see you soon.

Caitlin Ayer: All right.

Max Carpenter: Thank you.
summary:
**:date: Summary**

This was a product demonstration call between Beam AI (seller) and Ayer Electric (prospect) to review AI-generated takeoff results for an electrical project. The seller team, led by Anthony Anilesh Antic with support from Damini Gorania, walked through their software platform showing how AI had processed and measured electrical components from project drawings. The demonstration covered navigation features, measurement accuracy, export capabilities, and various output formats including Excel and PDF. The prospects, Caitlin Ayer and Max Carpenter, observed the walkthrough and expressed general satisfaction with the interface and functionality. The call concluded with scheduling a follow-up meeting for internal review and next steps discussion.

**:white_check_mark: Action Items**

- Anthony Anilesh Antic - Send email with platform link and Excel sheet (bill of materials) - Immediately after call
- Caitlin Ayer and Max Carpenter - Conduct internal review of AI takeoff accuracy against their own output - By April 8, 2026
- Caitlin Ayer and Max Carpenter - Review with larger team for potential next steps - By April 8, 2026
- Anthony Anilesh Antic - Send follow-up email to check on progress - By March 26 or March 30, 2026
- All parties - Reconvene for next steps discussion - April 8, 2026 at 11:00 AM

**:bust_in_silhouette: Prospect Info**

- Primary contacts: Caitlin Ayer and Max Carpenter from Ayer Electric
- Max Carpenter serves as the estimating expert leading a team of four estimators
- Ayer Electric handles diverse electrical projects, with complex commercial work requiring intensive takeoffs
- Company processes approximately 5 takeoffs monthly that would benefit from AI automation
- Target use case is specifically for complex commercial projects, not all bidding work requires detailed takeoffs

**:briefcase: Use Case**

- Seeking AI automation for tedious takeoff tasks on complex commercial electrical projects
- Want to evaluate overall accuracy level to determine if time savings justify implementation
- Need efficient solution for electrical component measurements and quantities from project drawings
- Looking to streamline intensive takeoff processes while maintaining accuracy standards

**:file_folder: Contract Status**

In evaluation phase - prospects will conduct internal accuracy review comparing AI output to manual takeoff results, discuss with larger team, then reconvene April 8, 2026 to determine next steps based on findings

**:speech_balloon: Customer Reactions**

- Software navigation - Max: 'It seems pretty straightforward to be able to navigate and do all that'
- Excel output format - Max: 'That that's some good information to have. It looks good'
- Overall demonstration - Both prospects expressed no concerns during walkthrough
- Process understanding - Max: 'I think that that all makes sense'

**:pill: Pain Points**

- Tedious manual takeoff tasks that consume significant time
- Need for more efficient processes specifically for complex commercial electrical project takeoffs

**:speaking_head: Product Discussion**

- Demonstrated AI-generated takeoff results with human verification layer for quality assurance
- Showed software interface with left-hand navigation for line items, quantities, and descriptions
- Explained page-specific vs takeoff aggregate views for individual sheets vs entire project summary
- Demonstrated locate measurement feature with red highlighting on drawings for precise component location
- Showed comprehensive export capabilities: shareable links (view-only), PDF, and Excel formats with customizable layouts
- Highlighted AI assumptions and notes feature with example of Cat 6 cable assumption for telecommunication outlets
- Demonstrated detailed measurements including Cat 6 cable for computer outlet at 3,955ft length
- Showed feeder identification and measurement capabilities with hover-over details
- Presented Excel output with categorized electrical items (lighting, wiring, power devices) including quantities, units, and sheet references

**:question: Questions Asked**

- Anthony Anilesh Antic (Seller): Does Max have a few estimators under him or is he literally the one man army here?
- Anthony Anilesh Antic (Seller): Among the four estimators, what is the total number of takeoffs that you guys are bidding on a monthly basis from a takeoff perspective?
- Anthony Anilesh Antic (Seller): So ideally, when do you think it makes sense for us to sort of reconvene?
- Anthony Anilesh Antic (Seller): In terms of the internal review, is there anything else that you would be reviewing besides accuracy?
- Max Carpenter (Prospect): Kaylin, what are your thoughts on that timeline for review?
- Caitlin Ayer (Prospect): Is it okay if we just reply to our email thread if we have questions along the way?

**:no_entry: Risks Obstacles**

- Accuracy validation requirement - AI output must meet their manual takeoff standards
- Internal team consensus needed - requires approval from larger team beyond the two attendees
- Time investment for thorough evaluation - minimum one week needed for comprehensive review

**:gear: Decision Process**

Multi-step collaborative evaluation: 1) Internal accuracy review by estimating team comparing AI output against manual takeoff results, 2) Discussion with larger organizational team about implementation potential, 3) Follow-up meeting on April 8, 2026 to discuss findings and determine next steps. Decision-making appears shared between Caitlin Ayer and Max Carpenter with broader team input required.

**:hourglass_flowing_sand: Timing**

Internal review timeline: Minimum one week needed for accuracy validation and team discussions. Follow-up meeting scheduled for April 8, 2026 at 11:00 AM (note: this appears to be the same date as the current call, suggesting potential scheduling confusion). Seller will send progress check email by March 26 or March 30, 2026.

action_items.task: Send email with platform link and Excel sheet (bill of materials)
Conduct internal review of AI takeoff accuracy against their own output
Review with larger team for potential next steps
Send follow-up email to check on progress

crm_fields.next_steps: Reconvene April 8 2026 after internal accuracy review
crm_fields.risks: Accuracy validation requirement; internal team consensus needed; time investment for evaluation
crm_fields.pain_points: Tedious manual takeoff tasks; need for efficient processes for complex commercial electrical projects
crm_fields.buying_signals: Positive reactions to software navigation and Excel output; agreed to internal review
